The location of the geniculate ganglion being part of the facial nerve further solidifies its connection to cranial nerve VII since this ganglion contains sensory neurons associated with pain and touch along the ear canal before they join the main trunk of the facial nerve.

**Answer:** A. Cranial Nerve VII (Facial Nerve) is indeed the cause of geniculate neuralgia.
